prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: In this quarter, the company's revenue hath grown by a most peculiar number, for it increased by exactly 37.5%, a fraction that bewildereth my quill. The knights of the Sales team, armed with their digital steeds, have conquered new clients from the far-flung lands of the internet, bringing forth a bounty of subscriptions. Yet, the expenses did not mirror this growth, for they remained as steady as a castle on a hill, unmoved by the winds of financial fluctuation. I must also note the curious phenomenon of "churn," where customers vanish like spirits, but the wise elders of the company assure me it is a common occurrence in these modern times.
